bark_sees_gun: "I got kids in this building. Just so you know."
"The way you're carrying that, something's going to happen today."
"Nah, I'm good, I'm walking the other way, have a good one."
"You got a permit for that or you got the other thing."
"Used to be you'd at least put it in a bag."
"I'm not looking, you're not here, we're both fine."
"My cousin caught one on this block. You mind moving along."
"Whatever you need it for, I hope it works out for you."
"That's a conversation I don't want to be part of. Excuse me."
bark_sees_blood: "You need a hospital or you need me to not ask."
"That's a lot of red for a Tuesday."
"Clinic on Myrtle's still open, if whatever happened didn't follow you."
"I got a towel. Don't argue, just take the towel."
"You walk past me bleeding and I'm supposed to do what, exactly."
"My sister's a nurse. Was. She'd tell you to sit down."
"The 36 still has a medic in the third car if you can make the platform."
"That happened to you or you happened to it."
"You're leaving a trail. Somebody's going to follow that."
"I'm not calling anyone. But somebody will."
"Worst I've seen since the seawall night. And you're walking. Good."
bark_high_heat: "Word came down fast. You got somewhere to be that isn't here."
"I don't know you. I especially don't know you right now."
"The corner knows. Which means the precinct knows. Which means go."
"Whatever you did, do it somewhere they're not already looking."
"My landlord calls them for a broken step. You think he won't call for this."
"I'm just saying there's a car been sitting there since you walked up."
"Run or don't run. Either way, do it somewhere else."
"They got the new face-match running on the bodega cameras. Just so you know."
"Heard your name twice already this morning. That's not good."
"I got warrants too. Let's both act like we're nowhere near each other."
bark_same_faith: "You keep the hours too. I can tell."
"Peace to you. Not a lot of us left on this block."
"I saw your mark. My grandmother wore the same one."
"We don't have to say anything. But it's good to see you."
"You're welcome at the table if you need a meal. No questions."
"Stay safe. I mean that the specific way, you understand."
"We used to meet on Thursdays. Place got flooded. We still meet."
"Blessing on you. And on whoever's waiting for you."
"Hard decade to keep faith. You're keeping it. That means something."
"If you need the words, I still have them. They didn't take the words."
bark_night: "Nothing good brought either of us out here at this hour."
"You see the sky tonight? That's not clouds. That's smoke from Jersey again."
"I used to be scared of this. Now it's just when I get things done."
"Stay in the light. The lights that still work."
"Third night running I've seen you out here. You sleeping?"
"Curfew's a suggestion now but the wrong people still enforce it."
"My kid thinks I work nights. I work nights."
"Be somewhere before four. Things that are out at four aren't going home."
"This used to feel dangerous. Now it just feels like the city."
"You want company to the next block or you're better off alone."
bark_rich: "You got that face people get when they stopped worrying about the small stuff."
"Whatever you're doing, don't tell me, I'll want to do it too."
"Dressed like that, you sure you're on the right block."
"People on this street are going to remember you. Make sure that's okay."
"Good for you. I mean it. Genuinely. How'd you manage it."
"You carry that well. Some people can't. You can."
"My mother used to say dress for the city you want. You're doing that."
"Keep moving. Looking like that, you're a target and a story simultaneously."
bark_broke: "I got half a dollar and I'm not going to need it today. Take it."
"You look like I looked in thirty-one. It passes. Slowly, but it passes."
"The kitchen on Flushing does a plate, no ID, until two."
"Don't tell me it's fine. It's not fine. That's okay, it's not fine."
"I got a couch if tonight's the bad kind of night."
"The decade did this to a lot of people. You're not the first I've seen."
"I'm not going to pretend I don't see you."
"There's work at the seawall if you can swing a bag of salt. Ugly work. Work."
"Eat something. Everything else is easier with something in you."
"You're still here. That counts. I'm not being cute, that actually counts."
bark_rain_omen: "Smells like the harbor. When it smells like the harbor, pay attention."
"This rain is different from regular rain. I know the difference now."
"My building's got a water line on the wall from thirty-three. I measure every storm."
"They said hundred-year flood. Then fifty-year. Now they stopped saying."
"I keep my papers on the third shelf. I moved them up after Queens."
"The old-timers knew by the birds. Birds still know. Watch the birds."
"City put in new drains on this block. They look nice. We'll see."
"Every time it rains like this, someone on my floor moves uptown."
"The decade had good years. Not many, but some. This isn't one."
"I'm not going inside until I see which way it's moving. You shouldn't either."
